overall_structure.description: The overall structure is a compact rocket with a central starting stone block. Four small wooden blocks are attached to the four side faces of the stone block, extending outward in the east, west, north, and south directions. Each small wooden block has a water cannon attached to its outer side face, oriented to point downward (negative z) for spraying steam downward, providing upward recoil. Each small wooden block also has a torch attached to its top face, oriented to point outward toward the respective water cannon, with the flame positioned to heat the water cannon's body without overlapping components. The physical dimensions ensure no overlaps: for example, the east small wooden block is centered at (1.5, 0, 0.5), its attached water cannon centered at (2.5, 0, 0.5) with body extending from z=-0.5 to z=1.25, and the torch body at (1.5, 0, 1.5) with flame at (2.5, 0, 1.5), placing the heating sphere (radius 0.3) intersecting the water cannon's inlet at z=1.25 (distance 0.25 < 0.3). Similar symmetric arrangements apply to the other directions, ensuring balanced thrust and no component overlaps based on block shapes and offsets.
overall_structure.functionality: The torches heat the water cannons, activating steam mode for increased recoil force (8.6 times the base 1.6 units per cannon, totaling approximately 55.04 units across four cannons). When activated, the water cannons spray steam downward, generating upward recoil that overcomes the structure's total mass of 11.45 units, causing the rocket to accelerate vertically upward. The symmetric placement ensures the center of thrust aligns with the center of mass for stable ascent.
overall_structure.motion_control: All water cannons are configured to the same control key. Pressing and holding this key activates all cannons simultaneously, producing constant upward thrust and resulting in vertical acceleration and ascent along the positive z-axis with minimal lateral deviation due to symmetry.

sub_structure_1.name: Steam Rocket
sub_structure_1.description: The structure features a central stone block with four outward-extending small wooden blocks on its side faces. Each wooden block supports a downward-oriented water cannon on its outer side and a laterally-oriented torch on its top, positioned to heat the cannon for steam propulsion.
sub_structure_1.components: 1 starting stone block, 4 Small Wooden Blocks, 4 Water Cannons, 4 Torches
sub_structure_1.motion_control: All water cannons respond to the same control key, activating together for balanced upward thrust and straight vertical motion.
sub_structure_1.function: This sub-structure serves as the complete rocket, providing heated steam propulsion for lift-off and ascent while maintaining stability.
sub_structure_1.design_requirements: Symmetric placement of components to ensure balanced thrust and minimal trajectory deviation; no overlaps between block bounding boxes (e.g., water cannon bodies extend vertically without intersecting other components); torches positioned to heat water cannons without igniting wooden blocks or being extinguished by steam. Positions may be micro-adjusted in later stages to resolve conflicts based on actual build execution.
